content: If we map the linguistic definition of reflection as both a mirror-like reflection of external input and intentional personal review onto the iterative LLM self-improvement pipeline of Self-Refine, we can reframe self-refinement for autonomous agents as a two-layered process: first reflecting the external task context to ground the agent's output, then reflecting on its own output to refine it. This reveals that existing approaches often prioritize the self-review layer and underutilize the contextual mirroring layer of reflection.
